title: oxalis san-miguelii
intro: Oxalis san-miguelii, also known as the Pink Wood Sorrel, is a perennial flowering plant native to the Mexican state of Guanajuato. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. Its leaves and stems are rich in vitamins, minerals, and flavonoids, which provide several health benefits such as reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Oxalis san-miguelii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as a remedy for various health issues.
